_doc: Source-of-truth registry for the market brief / live feed. One place where trust decisions live. Tiers: T1 authoritative/official, T2 reliable market data (unofficial), T3 context/color (never a signal). The 'news' block below is ACTIVE CONFIG read by fetch-feeds.js (fetchBroadMarket / fetchCryptoNews); the 'signals' block is the documented mapping of best-source-per-signal (the code paths are hardwired in regime engine.js / mood-signals.js / fetch-feeds.js — this table records the decisions). Updated 2026-06-19.

tiers.T1: Authoritative / official. Government, exchange, or canonical publisher. Trust the value.
tiers.T2: Reliable market data, unofficial endpoint. Cross-check where single-threaded.
tiers.T3: Context / color only. Noisy, gameable, or aggregator. Never drives a read.

signals.note: CNBC fallback + cross-check for equity/ETF tickers only; crypto/commodity stay Yahoo-only (CNBC symbology differs)
voter abstains if Yahoo fully down — single-value source can't rebuild a 20-day series
was CNN; CNN demoted to T3 display-only reference
Reuters/AP/FT/WSJ public RSS all checked 2026-06-19 — dead or paywalled or stale; none added. Quality gain here is tiering: trusted sources rank ahead of the aggregator.
context-only — removed from the mood composite 2026-06-19; gameable/noisy
demoted from regime voter to display-only reference 2026-06-19; black box, flaky here
